name: Hervor
description: Hervor is a shieldmaiden from Norse legend, featured prominently in the Hervarar saga ok Heiðreks. She is the daughter of Angantyr, a berserker king who was slain and buried with his cursed sword, Tyrfing. Driven by a desire for vengeance and glory, Hervor travels to her father's burial mound on Samsø island. There, she bravely confronts the spirits of her deceased relatives, demanding they relinquish Tyrfing. After a terrifying ordeal involving ghostly flames and threats from her ancestors, Hervor successfully retrieves the sword. She then disguises herself as a man, calling herself Hervard, and becomes a successful Viking warrior, amassing wealth and renown. Eventually, she tires of the warrior life, reveals her true identity, marries, and settles down. Her son, Heidrek, inherits Tyrfing, which brings misfortune and conflict to his reign. Hervor's story is significant because it showcases a woman actively defying societal expectations, embracing traditionally masculine roles, and wielding power in a patriarchal society. Her courage, determination, and the consequences of wielding a cursed weapon make her a compelling figure in Norse mythology, representing both heroic ambition and the dangers of unchecked power and fate.
subtitle: Shieldmaiden who claimed a cursed sword
achievements: Retrieving Tyrfing from her father's grave
Amassing wealth and renown as Hervard

enemies.name: The spirits of her deceased relatives
Fate and the curse of Tyrfing

keyMyths.title: Hervor's Awakening
keyMyths.description: Driven by a desire for vengeance, Hervor journeys to Samsø to claim her father's cursed sword. She confronts the ghosts of her ancestors to retrieve Tyrfing.

keyMyths.title: Hervard the Viking
keyMyths.description: Disguised as a man, Hervor becomes a renowned Viking warrior named Hervard. She amasses wealth and reputation through her strength and skill in battle.

keyMyths.title: The Curse of Tyrfing
keyMyths.description: Hervor's son, Heidrek, inherits Tyrfing, and the sword's curse brings misfortune and conflict to his rule. This highlights the destructive power of the weapon.

sources.author: Tolkien, Christopher (ed.)
sources.title: Hervarar Saga ok Heidreks
sources.description: A foundational text containing the primary account of Hervor's life and deeds, providing detailed descriptions of her encounters and lineage.

sources.author: Nora Kershaw Chadwick
sources.title: Studies on the Early German Hero Tale
sources.description: Scholarly analysis of Germanic hero tales, including Hervor's saga, examining themes of heroism, fate, and cultural significance.

associations.name: Tyrfing
associations.significance: Represents Hervor's inherited destiny, power, and the curse of her lineage.

associations.name: Samsø
associations.significance: The island where Angantyr's burial mound is located, symbolizing a place of ancestral connection and confrontation.

associations.name: Angantyr's Ghost
associations.significance: Represents the ancestral authority and the challenge Hervor must overcome to claim her inheritance.

associations.name: Shield
associations.significance: Symbolizes Hervor's protective nature and her role as a shieldmaiden, defending her honor and claiming her birthright.

associations.name: Courage
associations.significance: Hervor's bravery in facing the dead and claiming Tyrfing underscores her heroic qualities.

associations.name: Inheritance
associations.significance: Hervor's quest is driven by her desire to claim her father's sword, representing her lineage and destiny.

associations.name: Determination
associations.significance: Hervor's unwavering resolve in the face of supernatural opposition highlights her strong will.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Both Electra and Hervor are driven by a desire for vengeance and to reclaim a lost inheritance/honor, showcasing strong female characters seeking retribution.
Scáthach, a warrior woman, shares similarities with Hervor in their fierce independence and martial prowess, defying traditional gender roles.
Both Tomoe Gozen and Hervor are female warriors known for their exceptional combat skills and bravery on the battlefield.

worshipPractices.name: Norse Rituals
worshipPractices.description: While not directly worshipped, Hervor's story likely served as inspiration for Norse rituals honoring ancestors and celebrating bravery. Tales of shieldmaidens and strong female figures would have been shared during blóts (sacrifices) and feasts, reinforcing cultural values of courage and lineage.

cultural.modernLegacy: Hervor's character has inspired modern interpretations of Norse mythology in literature, film, and gaming, particularly in depictions of strong female warriors. Her story resonates with contemporary audiences interested in female empowerment and challenging traditional gender roles in historical and fantasy settings.

extendedContent.title: Early Life and Lineage
extendedContent.content: Hervor's early life is shrouded in mystery, but her parentage is well-defined within the sagas. She is the daughter of Angantyr, a formidable berserker king, and an unnamed mother. Angantyr possessed the cursed sword Tyrfing, forged by dwarves and destined to bring misfortune to its wielder and their kin. Upon Angantyr's death in battle, he was buried with Tyrfing on the island of Samsø. Hervor's upbringing is not explicitly detailed, but it is implied that she was aware of her father's reputation and the existence of the powerful, cursed sword. This knowledge fuels her later quest to retrieve Tyrfing and claim her inheritance. Her gender presents an initial obstacle, as societal expectations would not typically favor a woman inheriting such a weapon or seeking vengeance in the same manner as a male heir. However, Hervor's determination and courage set her apart, driving her to defy these expectations and embrace her destiny.

extendedContent.title: The Quest for Tyrfing
extendedContent.content: Hervor's most notable quest is her journey to Samsø to retrieve Tyrfing from her father's burial mound. Disguised as a man, she travels to the island and confronts the spirits guarding the mound. She summons her father, Angantyr, and other deceased relatives, demanding that they relinquish the sword. The spirits are initially reluctant, warning her of the sword's curse and the dangers it poses. However, Hervor persists, displaying unwavering courage and determination. Eventually, Angantyr yields to his daughter's demands and presents her with Tyrfing. Hervor, now in possession of the cursed sword, reveals her true identity and departs from Samsø. This quest highlights Hervor's bravery and her willingness to confront the supernatural to claim her birthright. The acquisition of Tyrfing marks a turning point in her life, setting her on a path of both glory and hardship, as the sword's curse begins to manifest in her life and the lives of those around her.

extendedContent.title: Legacy and Descendants
extendedContent.content: Hervor's legacy extends beyond her own exploits, as she becomes the ancestor of several prominent figures in Norse legend. She marries Hofund and has two sons, Heidrek and Angantyr the Younger. Heidrek inherits Tyrfing and becomes a powerful king, but the sword's curse continues to plague his reign, leading to conflict and tragedy. Angantyr the Younger is known for his wisdom and eventually avenges his brother's death. Through her descendants, Hervor's story becomes intertwined with the broader narrative of Norse kings and heroes. Her tale serves as a cautionary example of the dangers of ambition and the enduring consequences of curses. Nevertheless, she remains a symbol of female strength and determination, inspiring future generations with her courage and her willingness to defy societal norms. Her story also underscores the importance of lineage and the enduring impact of ancestral actions on the lives of their descendants.

shortDescription: Hervor is a fierce shieldmaiden from Norse legend who retrieves the cursed sword Tyrfing from her father's grave, defying expectations and embracing her destiny.
